data_gaps: route_participation and weighted opportunity (red-zone/end-zone usage) not retrievable -- needs nflverse participation/pbp, not pulled
injury status unavailable in frozen world
rookies with no NFL usage data (confidence capped): Jeanty, Hampton, Harvey, K.Johnson, Judkins, Henderson, Blue, Skattebo, Tuten
J.K. Dobbins absent from DEN depth chart (captured 2025-04-27, pre-signing)
Cam Skattebo absent from NYG depth chart (captured 2025-04-26)

rankings.rationale: 345 carries at 57.9% share (60.7% late) in the league's most run-leaning offense (41.9% pass, 62.9 plays/game). The largest secure touch pie in the pool; efficiency regressed, volume held.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: PHI pass rate above 50% in Weeks 1-3, or carry share below 50% healthy.

rankings.rationale: 61.4% carry share plus 14.1% target share at 75.4% snaps with nothing vacated and nothing added -- the pool's best dual-volume profile, fully returning.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Allgeier above 30% of carries early, or snap share under 65%.

rankings.rationale: Late-window share 50.4% at 25.0 PPG in the pool's highest-volume offense -- the trend my rules privilege -- capped by Montgomery's stable 41-42% committee half and a 55.3% snap share whose efficiency premium I regress.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Montgomery absent (up); snap share under 55% through Week 3 (down).

rankings.rationale: 73.7% carry share at 80.3% snaps -- the most monopolized backfield in the data, rising late (75.9%). Third pass weights the monopoly most: only the 7.5% target share keeps him out of the top three.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share above 10% early (up a tier); carry share below 60% (down).

rankings.rationale: 325 carries at 58.7% share in a 46.3%-pass offense; the 19.8-vs-16.9 season/late PPG gap on flat share is a TD-efficiency artifact I regress, and the 5.3% target share gives no PPR cushion.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 10+ carry/game committee partner in Baltimore.

rankings.rationale: 87 targets (15.3% share) plus a carry share rising to 53.9% late with 88 carries vacated by Mostert's exit -- in full-PPR the target volume alone anchors this tier.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share below 11% Weeks 1-3, or a 40%+ committee partner.

rankings.rationale: 301 carries at 57.2% share (58.1% late, 20.4 late PPG) in a 47.7%-pass offense with zero roster ambiguity -- volume continuity is the whole thesis.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Rotation pushes his share below 50% in the first month.

rankings.rationale: 83.0% carry share and 85.8% snaps over the final 8 -- near-monopoly usage worth 20.6 PPG in that window -- discounted for Cincinnati's 63.2% pass rate and Moss's return from the rotation.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Moss or a newcomer at 30%+ of early carries.

rankings.rationale: 86.4% snap share and 73.7% carry share on 316 carries -- third pass ranks the snap monopoly over the soft late window (15.3 PPG); the 7.9% target share is the ceiling cap.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Corum above 25% of carries early, or snap share below 75%.

rankings.rationale: The pool's best receiving volume (21.3% target share, 89 targets, stable late) plus 58.2% of carries -- in full-PPR that floor outranks the small-sample and trend cases this pass.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share below 15% Weeks 1-3, or a 40%+ committee.

rankings.rationale: Carry share 42.9%→48.3% with PPG rising to 18.3 in a 62-plays/game offense returning everything -- the cleanest ascending trend in the pool, weighted per my late-window rule.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: White out-snapping Irving in Weeks 1-2.

rankings.rationale: Elite shares in 2024 (54.3% carries, 17.9% targets, 72.3% snaps) but across only FOUR games after an injury-lost year, with injury status structurally unavailable to me -- this pass discounts the sample hardest. The 209 vacated carries keep the role his if healthy.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A healthy 65%+ snap share in Weeks 1-2 (up toward top 5); any camp absence (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (confidence capped) with the cleanest inherited-volume path in the pool: 233 vacated carries and an immediate RB1 listing over Mostert.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Committee signals with Mostert/White, or Week 1-2 carry share under 50%.

rankings.rationale: 72.4% late carry share at 85.6% snaps is bell-cow usage; Dowdle's arrival as listed RB2 in a 56.3-plays/game offense converts it to a projected majority split.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Dowdle above 35% of carries in the first month.

rankings.rationale: 66.9% carry share held identically across both windows with a 12.2% target share -- volume this stable is the methodology's favorite input; the late scoring dip (14.0) is efficiency, not role.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Chubb at 30%+ of carries in any healthy September week.

rankings.rationale: 61.1% carries / 14.1% targets / 72.4% snaps is top-10 volume; the pool's second-lowest team play volume (57.1/game) and mild late erosion set the discount.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Allen above 35% of early snaps.

rankings.rationale: Late window beat season (17.6 vs 15.9 PPG) on a rising 13.5% target share -- growing PPR-relevant volume with no real challenge from Benson's 19.1% share.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Benson above 30% of September carries.

rankings.rationale: 62.5% carry share and 13.7% target share across 11 games is top-10 per-game volume; the availability record and Charbonnet's proven 40%+ relief share are the discount.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Charbonnet above 40% of snaps with Walker healthy.

rankings.rationale: Committee usage (45.2% carries, 47.7% snaps, both lower late) whose 16.7 PPG rode touchdown efficiency my rules regress -- the touches, not the scoring, set this rank.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Week 1-3 snap share above 60% (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) atop 296 vacated carries but sharing with a 263-carry-durable veteran -- strong structure, committee base case.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Harris opening at 45%+ of carries (down 5+); 60%+ early share (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 42.2%/41.1% season/late carry share -- the most stable committee role in the data -- inside the pool's highest-volume offense; high-value usage my tools can't see does the scoring.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Detroit moving past a 60/40 split toward Gibbs.

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) inheriting the pool's largest vacated rush volume (376 carries) as listed RB1 in a 48.4%-pass offense -- this pass allocates inherited volume to listed rookies slightly more aggressively.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Warren opening as carry leader (down 5+); 60%+ share by Week 3 (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Secure volume (58.7% carries, 66.8% snaps, no listed threat), poor output (12.6 PPG) -- role-average efficiency on held volume lands just above his 2024 line; new-coordinator play volume is the stated unknown.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A new back at 30%+ of first-team camp carries.

rankings.rationale: 61.9% share on 260 carries -- top-12 volume in a bottom-tier environment (57.7 plays/game); volume is the signal, environment the cap.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Spears above 40%, or team play volume above 62/game (up).

rankings.rationale: Balanced 55.8%/12.2% shares over 17 games, but the traded-for Mason and 137 vacated carries signal a deliberate workload cut -- low-50s share projected with receiving intact.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Mason above 40% of September carries.

rankings.rationale: A 7-game injury year at a diminished 47.2% share leaves the rebound size unverifiable; the RB1 listing, KC's 61.8 plays/game, and 163 vacated targets frame the upside.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 55%+ carry share Weeks 1-2 (up 5+); even split with Hunt (down).

rankings.rationale: 45.3%→55.4% carry share with 66.8% late snaps as listed RB1 -- an ascending trend qualified by the drafted rookie invisible to my chart data.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Skattebo or Singletary above 35% of early carries.

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) behind Ford, but Cleveland's 258 vacated targets and 229 vacated carries are the pool's largest combined redistribution in a 61.9-plays/game offense -- this pass rates the structure over the chart order.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: RB1 usage by Week 1 (up 5+); three-way rotation (down).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) listed RB1 over 139 vacated carries -- but the Dobbins signing my tools cannot see makes Denver the least-verifiable backfield in the pool, and this pass weights that gap hardest.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Dobbins at 40%+ of Week-1 work (down 5+); clean RB1 September (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: An allocation of Dallas' 369 vacated carries to the listed RB1, against his own collapsed 17.2% late-2024 share -- structure over personal trend, Blue the claimant if it fails.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Blue at 40%+ of early carries (down 5+); 55%+ September share (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 53.1% share slipping to 48.8% late in a 57.4-plays/game offense that drafted Henderson -- a narrowing majority with both trend and draft signal pointing down.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Henderson held to change-of-pace work through September (up); an even split (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: 50.9% share and 14.75 PPG across 13 recent games is real volume; his absence from every chart in my data (DEN captured pre-signing) makes the 2025 role a stated gap, and Denver's 139 vacated carries the plausible fit.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Confirmed lead-back usage Week 1 (up 5+); clear backup role (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) RB2 with only 45 vacated carries to inherit -- share must be taken, not inherited, the harder path my methodology discounts.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing-down work plus 40%+ carries by October (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 263 durable carries at 49.3% share, now the minority side of a 296-vacated-carry backfield -- the pie is big enough that his floor survives the rookie.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Hampton at 65%+ of carries by October (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Fading carry share (34.5%→31.4%) with a preserved 11.3% target share -- PPR passing-down value in a 62-plays/game offense, decline priced in.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: An Irving absence (up 8+); losing passing downs (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Locked passing-down role (13.7% late target share) plus first claim on 376 vacated carries while the rookie settles -- stable floor, real early-season upside.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Warren opening as actual carry leader (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Listed RB1 with committee usage (42.6% carries) in the pool's lowest-volume offense, two challengers behind, new staff -- play volume unknowable (stated blind spot).
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 55%+ early share under the new staff (up 5+); split first-team work (down).

rankings.rationale: Two-down 41.7% share with sagging late output (8.7 PPG) and no passing-down role -- the same split projected forward in a good-volume offense.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share above 55% (up); a new early-down back (down).

rankings.rationale: 40.7% late relief share and 17-game durability behind an 11-game starter -- the most proven contingency in the pool, with capped standalone volume.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Walker injury or announced committee (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: 12.2% target share at 22.1% of carries -- a pure PPR passing-down profile with 118 vacated team targets as upside and 12 games played as the caveat.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing-down role shifting elsewhere (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Stable 29-31% share with dual usage behind a durable starter in a slow offense -- one injury from RB2 value, capped until then.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Pollard absence or 40%+ share week (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: 45.0% 2024 share fading to 29.4% late, traded into a backfield with 137 vacated carries -- revealed intent versus a declining trend, situation-change discount applied.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 40%+ of Minnesota's September carries (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) RB2 behind a weak incumbent in the pool's most volatile backfield (369 vacated carries) -- the cheap side of a genuine competition.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Leading the backfield in touches any September week (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Sole listed backup to a 4-game-2024 starter after 209 vacated carries consolidated the role -- the pool's highest-leverage handcuff.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Any McCaffrey absence (up 10+); a veteran signing (down).

rankings.rationale: 19.1% share with no growth trend behind a 236-carry starter -- pure handcuff on a stable ladder.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Conner absence (up 10+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie absent from my chart data entirely (NYG captured 2025-04-26) -- the lowest-information player in the pool, ranked on structural inference behind Tracy and Singletary.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Preseason usage ahead of Singletary (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Third slice of a three-way committee (23.0% carries) -- droppable depth whose value is entirely the promotion scenario.

evidence.value: Cook RB1, Johnson RB2, Davis RB3 (BUF)

rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Cook absence elevating him to 40%+ share (up 10+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) listed RB3 behind two incumbents in the pool's lowest-volume offense with little inherited volume -- the weakest opportunity structure among pool rookies.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing Bigsby on the usage ladder in September (up 5+).
